verdict: Routine graphic design — social media assets, logos, presentation decks — is being absorbed by generative tools fast. Brand strategy, art direction, and complex campaigns survive, but the entry-level path is shrinking and the pivot to creative direction matters more than ever.
timeline_callout: Production-design tasks have already collapsed in cost; the squeeze on junior designers is happening now and intensifies through 2028.

routine_cognitive.reasoning: Layout, color matching, and asset variants are increasingly templatable + AI-generatable.

creative_judgment.reasoning: Brand strategy and art direction stay human; production design does not.

stakes_of_error.reasoning: Most errors are reversible and reputational, not legal or physical.

relationships.reasoning: Client relationships matter, but mostly held by account leads / creative directors.

ai_capability_gap.reasoning: Midjourney, Firefly, and Canva AI handle a meaningful share of production output today.

key_tasks.task: Design layouts for print and digital media
key_tasks.note: Templated and AI-augmented — production volume per designer climbing fast.
AI generates options; humans choose and defend the system.
Generative image models compete directly here.

key_tasks.task: Present concepts to clients and revise based on feedback

key_tasks.note: Documentation gets AI assistance; enforcement and judgment do not.

evidence_ledger.meta: US Dept. of Labor · Updated Jan 2026
evidence_ledger.contribution: Task list shows production-heavy work mix.
Graphic designers score in the high-exposure band — visual generation is a top capability area.
Lists 'declining demand for routine design' explicitly.
Projects 3% decline — among the few creative roles BLS calls negative.
Marketing/communications is a top usage category — the buying side of design budgets.

reasoning_steps.title: Score the eight dimensions
reasoning_steps.body: routine_cognitive=7, ai_capability_gap=8, creative_judgment=7, regulatory_moat=0, embodied_work=0, stakes_of_error=2, relationships=4, physical_dexterity=0.

reasoning_steps.title: Calibration check
reasoning_steps.body: Anchor table lists Graphic Designer at 68. Computed 77 is 9 above. Soften toward the anchor — the creative_judgment lift is real for senior roles even if production work is exposed.

reasoning_steps.title: Confidence band
reasoning_steps.body: Medium ±9 — capability is moving fast and senior vs junior split makes a single number less precise than we'd like.

adjacent_careers.name: Art Director / Creative Director
adjacent_careers.reasoning: You direct AI tools instead of competing with them; judgment and taste are the moat.

adjacent_careers.name: UX / Product Designer
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Same visual sensibilities applied to interaction problems with more business judgment.

adjacent_careers.name: Industrial Designer
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Physical product design — embodied + spatial reasoning AI is far behind on.

adjacent_careers.name: Brand Strategist
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Strategy and storytelling stay human; you brief the designers and the AI.

adjacent_careers.name: Motion Designer / Animator
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Slightly more durable than static design; motion craft still has higher complexity.

action_plan.title: Build a portfolio that shows judgment, not just craft
action_plan.description: Case studies that explain why decisions were made beat polished mockups in the AI era.

action_plan.title: Learn one production AI tool deeply (Midjourney + Photoshop AI)
action_plan.description: Designers who direct the tools well are more valuable, not less, in the new pipeline.

action_plan.title: Take a course in UX or product design
action_plan.description: Adjacent specializations have lower exposure and similar starting trajectories.

action_plan.title: Get a real client (a school club, a small business)
action_plan.description: Working with a stakeholder you have to negotiate with builds the judgment muscles AI cannot replace.

follow_up_suggestions.label: Is industrial design a real pivot?
